## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

  ## Character
  Iterate-until-it-works — unknown-discovery zone, bounded. If cycle count
  exceeds ~3-4 without progress, wrap with findings and chivvy a fresh design
  pace rather than accreting.
  
  ## Docket
  Make garrison-w complete step 6 round-trip end-to-end against the
  committed code from the redesign+implement pace.
  
  Each cycle = mechanism + one targeted repair + run + diagnose. Not
  speculative cascading fixes.
  
  Done when:
  - tt/buw-jpGw.GarrisonWsl.sh bujn-winpc returns exit 0 with workload
    identity bujuw_user.
  - tt/buw-jwc and tt/buw-jws end-to-end operations succeed against the
    same node.
  - BUSJGW still coherent against implemented behavior.
  - Diag instrumentation decision recorded (kept-with-note vs pruned).
  
  Out of scope: c-letter (Cygwin) inherited defect — separate pace
  post-w-confirmation.

[0] rough (basis: 17f9a00)
    Silks: bcg-repair-bujb-cli

    ## Character
    
    Cleanup. Mechanical BCG conformance fixes on `Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh`.
    
    ## Docket
    
    Three classes of BCG drift in `bujb_cli.sh`, surfaced during review of
    the L3 commit that landed `bujb_command_file`. Scope is this single
    file; do not sweep siblings.
    
    1. **Output filenames as kindle constants.** `stdout.log`, `stderr.log`,
       `exitcode` are written as bare literals against `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.
       BCG Template 3 prescribes kindle constants for fixed paths under
       `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` (see the `Z«PREFIX»_OUTPUT_RESULT` example).
       Promote the three to `ZBUJB_OUTPUT_*` in `zbujb_kindle()` and
       update all call sites (the two ssh redirections, the exitcode
       write, and the `buc_die` message that references the stderr path).
    
    2. **Unguarded write.** The `echo "${z_exit}" > .../exitcode` line
       has no `|| buc_die` per the no-silent-failures discipline that
       neighbouring code in this same file follows.
    
    3. **Public command function entry shape.** The four public functions
       in this CLI (`bujb_resolve`, `bujb_knock`, `bujb_command_file`,
       `bujb_interactive_session`) skip the `zbujb_sentinel` first-line
       that BCG's boilerplate table mandates. Add the call. Do not audit
       sibling CLIs.
    
    Also: `local -r` pass on assigned-once locals in this file
    (`z_command_file` is the obvious one).
    
    Done when:
    - `bash -n Tools/buk/bujb_cli.sh` green
    - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green

[1] rough (basis: f6b094d)
    Silks: bcg-repair-bujb-cli

    ## Character
    
    Cleanup. Mechanical BCG conformance fixes on `Tools/buk/bujb*.sh`.
    
    ## Docket
    
    Sweep BCG drift across `bujb_cli.sh` and `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Scope
    is the bujb-prefixed module pair; do not sweep further siblings.
    
    Three classes of drift to address (verify in each file):
    
    1. **Output filenames as kindle constants.** `stdout.log`,
       `stderr.log`, `exitcode` in `bujb_cli.sh`, and any analogous
       bare-literal filename writes against `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` or
       `BURD_TEMP_DIR` not yet promoted. BCG Template 3 prescribes
       kindle constants for fixed paths. Promote to `ZBUJB_OUTPUT_*` /
       `ZBUJB_TEMP_*` in `zbujb_kindle()` and update call sites.
    
    2. **Unguarded write.** Any write/redirect missing the `|| buc_die`
       guard per the no-silent-failures discipline (e.g., `echo
       "${z_exit}" > .../exitcode`). Check both files.
    
    3. **Public command function entry shape.** Public `bujb_*`
       functions must call `zbujb_sentinel` as their first line.
       `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`'s public functions already conform; the
       CLI commands in `bujb_cli.sh` (`bujb_resolve`, `bujb_knock`,
       `bujb_command_file`, `bujb_interactive_session`) plus any new
       public entries introduced by prior paces (`bujb_privileged_ssh`-
       shaped) need the call.
    
    Also: `local -r` pass on assigned-once locals across the pair.
    
    Done when:
    - `bash -n` green on both files
    - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` green
    - `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green at baseline (no regression)

content.content:
1	## Goal
2	
3	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
4	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
5	Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
6	WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
7	integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
8	WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.
9	
10	## Current Concept
11	
12	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
13	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
14	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
15	Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
16	in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
17	
18	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
19	
20	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
21	  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
22	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
23	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
24	  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
25	  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
26	  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
27	  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
28	  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.
29	
30	**BUJB constant:**
31	
32	- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
33	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
34	  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.
35	
36	**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**
37	
38	- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
39	  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
40	  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
41	  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
42	  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
43	  admin-shell operations.
44	- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
45	  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
46	  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
47	  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
48	  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
49	  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
50	  precondition check.
51	- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
52	  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
53	  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
54	  authorized_keys.
55	
56	**Garrison-destructive model:**
57	
58	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
59	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
60	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
61	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
62	- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
63	  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.
64	
65	**Cross-cutting premises:**
66	
67	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
68	  it by reference, never duplicates.
69	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
70	  material; operator owns all key administration.
71	
72	**Tabtarget colophons (23 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
73	
74	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
75	- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
76	- `buw-jpI[WML]` — Invigilate per platform — 3
77	- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
78	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
79	- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
80	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
81	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
82	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
83	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
84	- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3
85	
86	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
87	
88	BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
89	implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
90	mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
91	privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
92	distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
93	hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
94	live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.
95	
96	**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**
97	
98	Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
99	admin posture, then post-completion verification.
100	
101	Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):
102	
103	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
104	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
105	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
106	   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
107	   process never sees the password.
108	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
109	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
110	   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
111	   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
112	3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
113	4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
114	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
115	5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
116	   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
117	   values.
118	6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
119	   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
120	7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
121	   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
122	   expected phase boundary).
123	
124	Phase 2 (key auth only):
125	
126	8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
127	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
128	   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
129	   a session.
130	9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
131	
132	Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):
133	
134	10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
135	    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
136	11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
137	12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
138	    (idempotent).
139	13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
140	    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
141	    wrong.
142	
143	**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**
144	
145	Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
146	ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
147	auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.
148	
149	**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**
150	
151	Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
152	systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
153	invigilate-linux post-check.
154	
155	**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**
156	
157	Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
158	fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
159	and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
160	posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
161	remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
162	garrison's precondition check.
163	
164	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**
165	
166	1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
167	   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
168	2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
169	   only (no password fallback).
170	3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
171	   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
172	   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
173	4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
174	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
175	5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
176	   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
177	6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
178	   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
179	   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
180	7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
181	
182	## Heat Sequence
183	
184	**Spec foundation:**
185	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
186	- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
187	  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
188	  caparison)
189	
190	**Implementation:**
191	- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
192	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
193	  superseded colophons + handbook entries
194	
195	**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
196	- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
197	  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
198	- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
199	  then macOS/Linux)
200	- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
201	  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)
202	
203	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
204	- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
205	
206	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
207	- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
208	- Garrison-WSL exercise
209	- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
210	- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)
211	
212	## Deferred (named for future return)
213	
214	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
215	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
216	  only.
217	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
218	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
219	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
220	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
221	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
222	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
223	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
224	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
225	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
226	- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
227	  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
228	  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
229	  caparison phase or sibling verb.
230	- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
231	  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
232	  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
233	  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
234	  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
235	  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
236	  a time, never both running concurrently).
237	- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
238	  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.
239	
240	## Standing Notes
241	
242	- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
243	  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
244	  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
245	  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
246	  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
247	  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
248	  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
249	  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
250	  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
251	  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
252	  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
253	- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
254	  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
255	  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
256	  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
257	  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
258	- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
259	  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
260	  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
261	  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
262	  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
263	  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
264	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
265	  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
266	  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
267	  privkey-at-rest protection.
268	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
269	  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
270	  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
271	  precondition.
272	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
273	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
274	  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
275	  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
276	- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
277	  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
278	  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
279	  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
280	  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
281	  daemon ever runs concurrently.
282	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
283	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
284	  and path construction.
285	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
286	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
287	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
288	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
289	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
290	  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
291	- **Mac Remote Login enabled is an operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN
292	  target** (including the operator's own machine). The first admin SSH
293	  must reach the host before caparison can do anything; only the operator
294	  can flip Remote Login on a fresh machine.
295	- **Linux nodes require `openssh-server` installed and `sshd` enabled** —
296	  operator-managed, not in caparison's scope.
297	- **Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is an operator-managed
298	  precondition** wherever garrison needs sudo elevation. On macOS,
299	  admin-group membership for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is also operator-
300	  managed (equivalent in posture to the ssh-copy-id prerequisite).
301	  Blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations; scoped
302	  is preferred for shared hosts.
303	- **Workload shell is `/bin/bash` on every platform** regardless of
304	  platform default. macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path —
305	  bash is the BUK execution substrate everywhere.

file.content:
## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.

**BUJB constant:**

- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.

**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**

- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
  admin-shell operations.
- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
  precondition check.
- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
  authorized_keys.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (23 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
- `buw-jpI[WML]` — Invigilate per platform — 3
- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.

**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**

Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
admin posture, then post-completion verification.

Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
   process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
   values.
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.

Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):

10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
    (idempotent).
13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
    wrong.

**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**

Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.

**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**

Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
invigilate-linux post-check.

**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**

Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
garrison's precondition check.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback).
3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
  caparison)

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
  superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
  then macOS/Linux)
- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
- Garrison-WSL exercise
- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  caparison phase or sibling verb.
- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
  a time, never both running concurrently).
- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.

## Standing Notes

- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
  precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
  daemon ever runs concurrently.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
  and path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
- **Mac Remote Login enabled is an operator prerequisite for any Mac BURN
  target** (including the operator's own machine). The first admin SSH
  must reach the host before caparison can do anything; only the operator
  can flip Remote Login on a fresh machine.
- **Linux nodes require `openssh-server` installed and `sshd` enabled** —
  operator-managed, not in caparison's scope.
- **Sudo NOPASSWD for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is an operator-managed
  precondition** wherever garrison needs sudo elevation. On macOS,
  admin-group membership for `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` is also operator-
  managed (equivalent in posture to the ssh-copy-id prerequisite).
  Blanket `NOPASSWD: ALL` acceptable on personal workstations; scoped
  is preferred for shared hosts.
- **Workload shell is `/bin/bash` on every platform** regardless of
  platform default. macOS ships /bin/bash 3.2 at the canonical path —
  bash is the BUK execution substrate everywhere.

content.text:
## Mounting ₢A-AAq (bcg-repair-bujb-cli)

**Pace goal**: Apply three classes of mechanical BCG conformance fixes across the `bujb_cli.sh` + `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` pair: (1) promote bare-literal output filenames (`stdout.log`, `stderr.log`, `exitcode`) to `ZBUJB_OUTPUT_*` kindle constants per BCG Template 3; (2) add missing `|| buc_die` guards on writes (the `echo "${z_exit}" > .../exitcode` line is the named culprit); (3) prepend `zbujb_sentinel` calls to public command functions (`bujb_resolve`, `bujb_knock`, `bujb_command_file`, `bujb_interactive_session`). Plus a `local -r` pass on assigned-once locals.

**Heat context**: ₣A- (rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures) — jurisdiction stack (BURN/BURP regimes + caparison/invigilate/garrison verbs) is implementation-complete; recent paces have been finishing the BUSJ spec family (BUSJCM/CL, BUSJIM/IL, BUSJHM/HL plus BUS0 sub-letter housekeeping for BUJP). This is a cleanup pace — BCG drift surfaced during review of the L3 commit that landed `bujb_command_file`.

**Two snapshots present**:
- `[0]` (basis: 17f9a00) — scoped to `bujb_cli.sh` only.
- `[1]` (basis: f6b094d) — broadened to the `bujb_cli.sh` + `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` pair, with `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` noted to already conform on the sentinel front. Snapshot [1] is the active docket.

**Note**: Next-actionable was ₢A-AA1 (empirical-garrison-w-workload-wsl, rough); you've routed to ₢A-AAq instead. The cleanup is independent — fine to take it first.

**Name check**: silks `bcg-repair-bujb-cli` is now slightly narrower than the [1] scope (which includes `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`). Possible rename to `bcg-repair-bujb-pair` or similar. Not urgent.

**Approach** (4 bullets):
- Audit both files for the three drift classes; locate exact line-sites for kindle constants, unguarded writes, and missing sentinel calls.
- Promote the three filenames to `ZBUJB_OUTPUT_*` in `zbujb_kindle()`, update call sites (two ssh redirections + exitcode write + `buc_die` message referencing stderr path).
- Add `|| buc_die` guards on unguarded writes; sweep for any beyond the named one.
- Add `zbujb_sentinel` first-line to the four (plus any new public entries); `local -r` pass on assigned-once locals.
- Gate: `bash -n` both files, `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh`, `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`.

**Execution strategy**: Sequential sonnet — mechanical BCG conformance, two related files, well-scoped. No parallelization warranted (the three drift classes interact within the same functions; need linear edits).

Proceed?

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
20	#
21	# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
22	# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
23	# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
24	# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.
25	
26	set -euo pipefail
27	
28	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Command Functions
32	
33	# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
34	# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
35	bujb_resolve() {
36	  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
37	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
38	
39	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
40	
41	  bujb_resolve_investiture
42	
43	  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
44	  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
45	  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
46	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
47	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
48	  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
49	  buc_bare "  BUJB_workload_user      = ${BUJB_workload_user}"
50	}
51	
52	# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
53	bujb_knock() {
54	  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
55	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
56	
57	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
58	
59	  bujb_resolve_investiture
60	
61	  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"
62	
63	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
64	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
65	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
#
# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions

# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
bujb_resolve() {
  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
  buc_bare "  BUJB_workload_user      = ${BUJB_workload_user}"
}

# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
bujb_knock() {
  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Knocking ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || buc_die "Knock failed for ${BUZ_FOLIO}"

  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
}

# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
bujb_command_file() {
  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local z_command_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}"
  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      'bash -s'                                   \
      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
      > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stdout.log"           \
      2> "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"          \
    || z_exit=$?

  echo "${z_exit}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/exitcode"

  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"

  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
  buc_step "Capture complete"
}

# bujb_fenestrate - fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only).
bujb_fenestrate_command() {
  buc_doc_brief "Fenestrate — admin SSH key trust + sshd_config harden (Windows)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_fenestrate
}

# bujb_privileged_ssh_command - run an arbitrary command as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER.
# Pass-through: argv joined into ssh's remote-command position; no shell wrapping.
bujb_privileged_ssh_command() {
  buc_doc_brief "Privileged SSH — run a command on a node as BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER"
  buc_doc_param "command..." "Command tokens executed on the remote node (pass-through)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  test $# -ge 1            || buc_usage_die

  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_privileged_ssh "$@"
}

# bujb_wsl_install_command - idempotently provision the canonical WSL distro.
bujb_wsl_install_command() {
  buc_doc_brief "WSL Install — provision canonical WSL distribution from Ubuntu-24.04 seed (Windows, idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_wsl_install
}

# bujb_garrison_bash - garrison ceremony, native bash workload shell (Linux/Mac).
bujb_garrison_bash() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_linux/bubep_mac node (shell-letter b)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison b
}

# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_cygwin() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via Cygwin (shell-letter c)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison c
}

# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
bujb_garrison_wsl() {
  buc_doc_brief "Garrison workload account on a bubep_windows node via WSL (shell-letter w)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
  bujb_resolve_investiture
  bujb_garrison w
}

# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
bujb_interactive_session() {
  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  bujb_resolve_investiture

  buc_step "Opening interactive session: ${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST} (${BUZ_FOLIO})"

  exec ssh -t                                     \
      -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      "${BUJB_workload_user}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      "bash -i"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zbujb_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bubc_constants.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burp_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buwz_zipper.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujb_jurisdiction.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bujp_preflight.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle
  zbuz_kindle
  zbuwz_kindle
  zbujb_kindle
  zbujp_kindle

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio

  local -r z_burp_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir}/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burp.env"
  test -f "${z_burp_file}" || buc_die "BURP profile not found: ${z_burp_file}"
  source "${z_burp_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURP: ${z_burp_file}"
  zburp_kindle
  zburp_enforce

  # Cross-reference: investiture name (BUZ_FOLIO) must equal a registered investiture.
  # The 1:1 constraint is enforced by file presence — the BURN profile directory
  # name IS the investiture, and it must match BUZ_FOLIO exactly.
  local -r z_burn_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_burn_file}" || buc_die "Investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO} has no matching investiture (BURN profile not found): ${z_burn_file}"
  source "${z_burn_file}"  || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_burn_file}"
  zburn_kindle
  zburn_enforce
}

buc_execute bujb_ "Bash Utility Jurisdiction" zbujb_furnish "$@"

# eof

1511:    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"
1568:  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"
1572:      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT}"     \
1573:      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR}"     \
1583:  zbujb_sentinel
1591:      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"       \
1592:      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"       \
1593:    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart or sshd did not come back up; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"
1598:  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"
1607:  zbujb_sentinel
1608:  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
1625:  zbujb_sentinel
1636:  zbujb_sentinel
1637:  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
1712:  zbujb_sentinel
1713:  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
1722:      "${ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS[@]}"                \

200	BUJB_ps_prelude="\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'; \$env:WSL_UTF8 = 1; \$LASTEXITCODE = 0;"
201	
202	# PowerShell-form path to the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config. Bash-side
203	# substitution at the use site expands this tinder, baking the literal
204	# $env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config into the constructed PS body.
205	BUJB_ps_sshd_config_path='$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config'
206	
207	# Windows registry subpath under HKLM for the per-SID ProfileList key
208	# created by step3 w to register the workload user's profile with Windows.
209	# Two consumers prefix it differently: PS-drive form 'HKLM:' for
210	# New-Item / New-ItemProperty, and reg.exe hive form 'HKLM' for the
211	# validate-diag dump.
212	BUJB_winreg_profilelist_subpath='SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList'
213	
214	######################################################################
215	# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)
216	
217	zbujb_kindle() {
218	  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"
219	
220	  # Fenestrate temp file paths — ssh stdout/stderr captured here so
221	  # callers parse from disk (no `$(ssh ...)` capture).
222	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stdout.txt"
223	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stderr.txt"
224	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stdout.txt"
225	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stderr.txt"
226	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stdout.txt"
227	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stderr.txt"
228	
229	  # stat captures (per-key-slot for parallel forensics on resolve).
230	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stdout.txt"
231	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stderr.txt"
232	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stdout.txt"
233	  readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stderr.txt"
234	
235	  # ssh-keygen dry-load captures (resolve-time, per slot).
236	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stdout.txt"
237	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stderr.txt"
238	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stdout.txt"
239	  readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stderr.txt"
240	
241	  # ssh-keygen pubkey-emit captures (garrison step4 = workload pubkey,
242	  # fenestrate phase1 = admin pubkey).
243	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work_stdout.txt"
244	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work_stderr.txt"
245	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv_stdout.txt"
246	  readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv_stderr.txt"
247	
248	  # base64 capture (garrison step4 w-branch = authorized_keys line b64-encoded
249	  # for transport to remote).
250	  readonly ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_authkeys_b64_stdout.txt"
251	  readonly ZBUJB_AUTHKEYS_B64_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_authkeys_b64_stderr.txt"
252	
253	  # Obliterate per-call captures (reused across the Windows sub-step
254	  # sequence — last call's content is what's preserved at die time).
255	  readonly ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_obliterate_stdout.txt"
256	  readonly ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_obliterate_stderr.txt"
257	
258	  # Per-call captures for zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust.
259	  readonly ZBUJB_PLACE_TRUST_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_place_trust_"
260	
261	  # Per-call captures for zbujb_garrison_step6_validate.
262	  readonly ZBUJB_VALIDATE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_validate_"
263	
264	  # Per-call captures for the garrison-w SSH-as-workload phases
265	  # (export-seed, init-wsl, lockdown, seed-cleanup).
266	  readonly ZBUJB_W_INIT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_w_init_"
267	
268	  # Per-call forensic capture for obliterate sub-steps (counter pattern,
269	  # like the other _run wrappers). Each ssh call lands at a uniquely
270	  # numbered file, so probe and destructive ops both leave evidence.
271	  readonly ZBUJB_OBLITERATE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_obliterate_run_"
272	
273	  # Single shared emission counter across all _run wrappers — file numbers
274	  # are continuous across originating functions so chronological order is
275	  # readable from the embedded number even when prefixes differ.
276	  z_bujb_emit_index=0
277	
278	  # SSH base options shared by every ssh invocation in this module:
279	  # IdentitiesOnly pins the key to ssh -i; StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new
280	  # records first-contact host keys without prompting. The dominant
281	  # BatchMode=yes / ConnectTimeout=15 pair lives in tinder constants
282	  # (BUJB_ssh_opt_batchmode_yes, BUJB_ssh_opt_connecttimeout_15) referenced
283	  # inline at each ssh — security review reads one source-of-truth
284	  # declaration. PreferredAuthentications and the variant cases
285	  # (BatchMode=no for password-fallback, ConnectTimeout=10 for knock) stay
286	  # inline at their lone call sites.
287	  ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS=(
288	    -o IdentitiesOnly=yes
289	    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new
290	  )
291	  readonly ZBUJB_SSH_BASE_ARGS
292	
293	  readonly ZBUJB_KINDLED=1
294	}
295	
296	zbujb_sentinel() {
297	  test "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bujb not kindled - call zbujb_kindle first"
298	}
299	
300	# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME SLOT -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
301	# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
302	# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label. SLOT
303	# (priv|work) selects the kindle temp-file pair used for stat + dry-load
304	# stdout/stderr capture so failures preserve forensic evidence per BCG.
305	zbujb_check_key_file() {
306	  local z_path="${1:-}"
307	  local z_varname="${2:-}"
308	  local z_slot="${3:-}"
309	
310	  test -n "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
311	  test -f "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"
312	
313	  local z_stat_stdout="" z_stat_stderr="" z_dry_stdout="" z_dry_stderr=""
314	  case "${z_slot}" in
315	    priv)
316	      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV}"
317	      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV}"
318	      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV}"
319	      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV}"
320	      ;;
321	    work)
322	      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK}"
323	      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK}"
324	      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK}"
325	      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK}"
326	      ;;
327	    *)
328	      buc_die "zbujb_check_key_file: invalid slot (expected priv|work): '${z_slot}'"
329	      ;;
330	  esac
331	
332	  # BSD stat first (Mac), GNU stat fallback (Linux). The failed flavor's
333	  # stderr is overwritten by the successful flavor's invocation; on total
334	  # failure z_stat_stderr holds the GNU-attempt error.
335	  if stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
336	    : # macOS / BSD stat succeeded
337	  elif stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
338	    : # GNU/Linux stat succeeded
339	  else
340	    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path} — see ${z_stat_stderr}"
341	  fi
342	
343	  local z_mode
344	  z_mode=$(<"${z_stat_stdout}")
345	  z_mode="${z_mode//$'\n'/}"
346	  test "${z_mode}" = "600" \
347	    || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"
348	
349	  ${BUJB_sshkeygen_emit_pubkey} "${z_path}" \
350	      > "${z_dry_stdout}" \
351	      2> "${z_dry_stderr}" \
352	    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path} — see ${z_dry_stderr}"
353	}
354	
355	######################################################################
356	# Public Functions (bujb_*)
357	
358	# bujb_resolve_investiture -- sole runtime cross-validation entrypoint.
359	#
360	# Preconditions: BURC, BURN, BURP regimes already kindled and enforced
361	# (the bujb_cli furnish handles this for tabtarget callers; the cli's
362	# BURP and BURN sourcing also implicitly cross-validates that the
363	# investiture name (BUZ_FOLIO) equals a registered investiture —
364	# enforcement is by file presence of the matching BURN profile).
365	#
366	# Behaviour: validates BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE and BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
367	# (exist, mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load proves parseable + unencrypted) —
368	# the validation regime enforce cannot do because it requires running
369	# ssh-keygen against the private key. After validation, sets ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
370	# as the I-have-been-validated sentinel. Callers reference the regime vars
371	# directly (BURP_*, BURN_*, BURC_*); no separate published namespace.
372	# Single-call-per-process; subsequent calls die.
373	#
374	# BCG deviation: ZBUJB_RESOLVED is a kindle-shaped (SCREAMING + readonly)
375	# constant set outside zbujb_kindle. The deviation is load-bearing —
376	# resolve-investiture runs ssh-keygen dry-loads against private keys, which
377	# kindle cannot do (kindle establishes module state at sourcing time, not
378	# after BURP regime enforcement). This matches the regime archetype's
379	# "enforce after kindle, lock after enforce" pattern (BCG line 113); we
380	# just spell the lock as a discrete sentinel because there's no enrolled-
381	# variable set to readonly in this module.
382	bujb_resolve_investiture() {
383	  zbujb_sentinel
384	  zburc_sentinel
385	  zburn_sentinel
386	  zburp_sentinel
387	
388	  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
389	    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"
390	
391	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE" priv
392	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"   work
393	
394	  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
395	}
396	
397	# bujb_command_for_capture SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
398	# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w. _capture form so callers
399	# may use `$()` per BCG line 502; returns 1 on invalid letter, callers must
400	# `|| buc_die`. All tinder-on-tinder interpolation already resolved at
401	# source time — uniform letter-case, no per-letter substitution gymnastics.
402	bujb_command_for_capture() {
403	  zbujb_sentinel
404	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
405	  case "${z_letter}" in
406	    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
407	    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
408	    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
409	    *) return 1 ;;
410	  esac
411	}
412	
413	######################################################################
414	# Internal: Garrison helpers
415	
416	zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
417	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
418	  case "${z_letter}" in
419	    b|c|w) ;;
420	    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
421	  esac
422	}
423	
424	# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BURN_PLATFORM
425	# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
426	zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
427	  zbujb_sentinel
428	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
429	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
430	  case "${z_letter}" in
431	    b)
432	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
433	        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
434	        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
435	      esac
436	      ;;
437	    c|w)
438	      test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
439	        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
440	      ;;
441	  esac
442	}
443	
444	# zbujb_workload_home_capture LETTER -- emit the absolute workload home path
445	# on the remote node for the given shell-letter. _capture form per BCG line
446	# 502; returns 1 on unsupported platform or invalid letter, callers must
447	# `|| buc_die`.
448	zbujb_workload_home_capture() {
449	  zbujb_sentinel
450	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
451	  case "${z_letter}" in
452	    b)
453	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
454	        bubep_linux) echo "${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}" ;;
455	        bubep_mac)   echo "${BUJB_path_mac_user_home}"   ;;
456	        *)           return 1 ;;
457	      esac
458	      ;;
459	    c|w) echo "${BUJB_path_posix_user_home}" ;;
460	    *)   return 1 ;;
461	  esac
462	}
463	
464	# zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl} STMT -- run a single bash statement as
465	# the privileged user on the remote node, in the named shell environment.
466	# Variant functions per platform (no cryptic letter parameter): the call
467	# site picks the shell by name, and the function selects the matching
468	# remote invoker (`bash`, cygwin's bash --login, or wsl.exe ... bash).
469	# Embedded " characters are escaped to \" so the outer "..." of
470	# `bash -c "..."` survives the cmd.exe / Windows argv-parser layer
471	# (relevant for cygwin/wsl variants via Windows OpenSSH).
472	#
473	# Single-statement contract per WSG SH-10: STMT must be exactly one of
474	# (1) one simple command with arguments and redirections, (2) one pipeline
475	# optionally preceded by `set -o pipefail`, or (3) one simple command
476	# whose argument is itself an inner-shell body. Multi-statement work
477	# decomposes into multiple round-trips per the Capture-Decide-Dispatch
478	# pattern; bash on the curia owns the state machine.
479	#
480	# Caller may pipe stdin into any variant (e.g.
481	#   `zbujb_admin_exec_native "install ... /dev/stdin '<target>'" < <file>`)
482	# — bash redirection attaches the file to FD 0, ssh inherits it, sshd
483	# forwards it to the remote command's FD 0, the body's single command
484	# reads from /dev/stdin. Used by garrison step5 to plant the workload
485	# key without ever materializing the plaintext on either side's disk.
486	#
487	# Caller redirects stdout/stderr for capture; returns ssh exit code.
488	# These functions do NOT transform $. Body authors are responsible for
489	# per-shell $-escape discipline (see WSG SH-6: only the wsl variant's
490	# bodies need `\$name` escaping).
491	zbujb_admin_exec_native() {
492	  zbujb_admin_exec_impl 'bash' "$@"
493	}
494	
495	zbujb_admin_exec_cygwin() {
496	  zbujb_admin_exec_impl "${BUJB_path_cygwin_root_fwd}/bin/bash --login" "$@"
497	}
498	
499	zbujb_admin_exec_wsl() {
500	  zbujb_admin_exec_impl "wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash" "$@"
501	}
502	
503	# zbujb_admin_exec_impl REMOTE_INVOKER STMT -- shared body for the three
504	# zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl} variants. Not called directly by
505	# step code; callers always go through one of the named variants so the
506	# call site declares its target shell.
507	zbujb_admin_exec_impl() {
508	  zbujb_sentinel
509	  local z_remote_invoker="${1:-}"
510	  test -n "${z_remote_invoker}" \
511	    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_exec_impl: REMOTE_INVOKER required (call via zbujb_admin_exec_{native,cygwin,wsl})"
512	  shift
513	  test $# -eq 1 \
514	    || buc_die "zbujb_admin_exec_*: requires exactly one statement (got $#); decompose multi-statement work via Capture-Decide-Dispatch per WSG SH-10"
515	  local z_body="$1"
516	
517	  # Escape " → \" for the cmd.exe / Windows argv-parser layer.
518	  local z_body_escaped="${z_body//\"/\\\"}"
519
